content: Got I2C in a stat, that I committed my first WIP commit. Still working on it, but if anyone wants to take a look: https://github.com/nyantec/linux/tree/rust-i2c
Created a PR for the basic module macro: https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/946

Hey Finn, appreciate the effort. Can you please share the currently supported APIs ? A small Quickstart would be really helpful.

Thank you,
Sarbojit
Don’t have that much right now. Currently setting up my hardware, so I can check if the work correctly. Best look at the source for the functions that I did wrap already

I also started to test this today. I am having struggle getting my module to be probed... You sounded like you are testing with real hardware (versus QEMU)? So I guess there is little that I can try to replicate in order to rule out stupid mistakes that I did?
Yes, the current code is only how I think it should be, and I have a specific hardware that I plan to use this on

@Finn Behrens: I think registrations still need something like https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/rust/drivers/gpio/gpio_pl061_rust.rs#L292 in order to safely be able to keep an i2c_device reference so that one can use it outside of probe(), or am I missing some trick how this could work with the current PR? But I tested it and was able to read things from within a probe function fine :)
That could be, not yet fully tested it. Also my first take on the I2C Subsystem
Hi @Finn Behrens, I want in the near future to start working on a driver for an I2C device. Are you still working on your I2C rust wrappers?

Sadly currently not actively, as I still don’t have the proper hardware that I’m supposed to work on yet, and so got given some other (non kernel related task). But feel free to be inspired by what I had, and also I can probably get some time to help if that is of service

If anyone is interested, I ported @Finn Behrens's PR to rust-dev: https://github.com/Fabo/linux/tree/fparent/rust-i2c.
I also added of match table support, and a sample.

I tested the abstraction, and it seems to be working fine.

Fabien Parent said:

If anyone is interested, I ported Finn Behrens's PR to rust-dev: https://github.com/Fabo/linux/tree/fparent/rust-i2c.
I also added of match table support, and a sample.

I think this is what I was looking for in https://rust-for-linux.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/288089-General/topic/Platform.20device.20and.20devicetree.20abstraction.3F

So many thanks for this!

sorry just found this... i've been writing a rust i2c stub against raspberry pi's fork of stable.

i'm keen to use finn's wrappers but need to rework my dev environment somewhat first. i haven't been able to use anything other than the raspberry pi fork yet.

also just wanted to say many thanks for getting ARM support into stable (because thus far that has been my only avenue to access RfL)
